children.heading: .—Exclusive Appellate Jurisdiction
children.label: a .—Exclusive Appellate Jurisdiction
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit shall have exclusive jurisdiction to determine the validity of a final judgment rendered by a military commission (as approved by the convening authority and, where applicable, as affirmed or set aside as incorrect in law by the United States Court of Military Commission Review) under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Exhaustion of Other Appeals
children.label: b .—Exhaustion of Other Appeals
children.text: The United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit may not review a final judgment described in subsection (a) until all other appeals under this chapter have been waived or exhausted.
not later than 20 days after the date on which written notice of the final decision of the United States Court of Military Commission Review is served on the parties; or

if the accused submits, in the form prescribed by , a written notice waiving the right of the accused to review by the United States Court of Military Commission Review, not later than 20 days after the date on which such notice is submitted.section 950c of this title

children.heading: .—Scope and Nature of Review
children.label: d .—Scope and Nature of Review
children.text: The United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit may act under this section only with respect to the findings and sentence as approved by the convening authority and as affirmed or set aside as incorrect in law by the United States Court of Military Commission Review, and shall take action only with respect to matters of law, including the sufficiency of the evidence to support the verdict.

children.heading: .—Review by Supreme Court
children.label: e .—Review by Supreme Court
children.text: The Supreme Court may review by writ of certiorari pursuant to the final judgment of the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit under this section.section 1254 of title 28
